spec_match.rationale: Uses calc(anchor-size(width) * -0.28) and calc(anchor-size(height) * -0.28) inside top/right — the textbook inset-with-anchor-size pattern the spec change enables.

interactive.rationale: Small/Medium/Large buttons rescale every icon button, badge follows proportionally. Side-by-side comparison versus hardcoded `-8px` shows the drift. Click-to-decrement on badges and Reset bring real state.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises a size slider + a drift comparison vs hardcoded offsets, which is what ships.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers small/medium/large neatly. But the @supports unsupported banner uses the rule `(top: anchor-size(width))` which is the right test for inset but won't trigger on a browser that has SIZING-only anchor-size() — would be more honest with `@supports (top: anchor-size(width)) and (not (margin: 0))` style negative test. Also no demo of right-to-left flip where right/left swap.

design_system.rationale: The unsupported banner declares `background: var(--accent-amber, #b87b00)` — raw hex fallback inside the CSS variable lookup. Either the variable is defined globally (then drop the fallback) or it's not (then add it to the design system). Multiple inline `style=...` attributes on `<div>` wrappers in the badge demo break separation of concerns but cosmetically only.

openQuestions.title: Drop or document the raw-hex fallback in --accent-amber
openQuestions.detail: `var(--accent-amber, #b87b00)` carries a raw hex that violates rule #5. Either confirm `--accent-amber` is defined in public/styles.css (and drop the fallback) or formally add it to the design system. Same colour is repeated across many v131 concepts — handle once.

openQuestions.title: Add an RTL preset
openQuestions.detail: In RTL the badge should pin to the LEFT corner. `inset-inline-end: calc(anchor-size(width) * -0.28)` works automatically, but the current page hardcodes `right`. Add a `dir=rtl` toggle on the stage so visitors see the i18n implication.

openQuestions.title: Move inline styles to classes
openQuestions.detail: Several wrapping <div>s use `style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;..."`. These should be a `.btn-stack` class. Not a regression but cleaner.

summary: Easily the strongest concept in this feature: real comparison vs hardcoded offsets, real interactive count state, real size variants. Minor polish needed around the amber colour fallback and an i18n preset.
